title: Hook
text: Hey guys. I had this idea for a bullet hell that's based off of Realm of the Mad God, Legends of Yore, Morrowind, and a bunch of old-school games, but I want to modernize it a bit. It’s just me working on this alone with no real plan. Honestly, uh... I’m just gonna keep building and see how far I can push it before the tech debt starts fighting back. This is devlog one, I guess.

title: Intro
text: So, basically, it’s a 2D open-world thing. Dungeons, crafting, traps... all that. I’ve, uh, I’ve wanted to build this specific loop for a long time, so I’m finally just... I'm just building it. I think I'm calling it *Eternal Conquests* for now. We'll see if that, uh... if that sticks.

title: Physics
text: I, uh... I spent way too long on the physics. Like, way too long. I ended up with, uh, four weight classes—light to super heavy. It’s on the minions and the bosses too, so it isn't just a, you know, a floaty bullet hell. You actually feel the... the momentum when you move.

title: VFX
text: I, uh, I added these trailing dash lines here. I think they, um... they look okay, actually.

title: The Dungeon
text: So, this is the main dungeon loop. Traps, corridors... some bloodstains and skulls for the, uh, for the atmosphere. You know, the good stuff.

title: Lighting
text: The, uh... the dynamic lighting. That’s probably what I’m happiest with, honestly. I, um... I made it dark on purpose. I might do, uh, "inverse" lighting later—kind of like that *Samurai Jack* shinobi scene where everything is white? Could be interesting.

title: Treasure
text: Treasure room. Just, uh... just health pots and some rare drops in here for now.

title: Testing
text: I’ve, uh... I’ve cleared this place like five times already with this character. So, yeah... they’re pretty overpowered at this point.

title: Procedural Gen
text: The, um... the proc-gen. It uses specific corridor and room logic. I didn't want a random, uh, a random mess of boxes. I wanted it to feel like an actual maze you have to, you know, actually navigate.

title: Length
text: Wait, this... this actually goes on for a while. I should probably, uh... I should probably make the corridors shorter. Or just, you know, dial back the room count.

title: Elites
text: So, these are elite enemies. Basically mini-bosses. They have, uh... they have better gear drops and more aggressive movement. Nothing too crazy yet, though.

title: Balancing
text: Wait, look at this—this guy's just, uh... he's spamming way too many bombs. I definitely need to, um... I need to fix that.

title: Stats & Armor
text: The traps... they’re supposed to deal pure damage and just, uh, ignore armor. I have this Vitality stat for the, uh, for the HP regen, but it’s... honestly, it's completely broken right now. Way too overpowered. Like, seriously.

title: Rare Rooms
text: Rare decorated room. These, uh... they spawn enemies that are basically bosses. But they’re rare, so, um... probably won't see one here.

title: Progression
text: The floors... they just keep descending until you hit the boss. I might, uh... I might add an infinite mode later that just, you know, loops forever.

title: Hallways
text: I, uh... I put enemies in the corridors to patrol the halls. I’m thinking about a, um... a sneaking system, too. So you don't always have to just, you know, shoot your way through everything.

title: Inventory & Stats
text: Inventory is still—actually, wait, there’s an enchanting system in here. It technically shouldn't even be in this build yet, but, uh... whatever. On the right is where you, um... where you apply points to Agility or Power.

title: Items & Movesets
text: The, uh... the gear actually changes your moveset on Shift and Spacebar. Like, the shortbow lets you kite with a, uh, a triple shot that pushes you back... but the longsword lets you just, you know, dash in and shove enemies.

title: The Boss
text: And, uh... here’s the boss. This is where I've been using Claude Code and some custom codex scripts to get the fractal bullet patterns working. The idea was to have this, um, mathematically beautiful chaos... but right now he just kind of panics and shoots in random directions. I genuinely don’t know why. So, yeah. Work in progress.

title: Height System
text: Top-down games... they always feel so flat. Everything's the same level. So I’m using, uh... I'm using shaders and art to give the world actual elevation. I’ll use this for, you know, fortified hills where you’re fighting uphill against archers.

title: Outro
text: So, yeah. That’s pretty much it, I guess. A lot of bugs still, and the loop isn't... well, it isn't fully tight yet. But the bones are good. Anyway, um... if you’re into this, subscribe. It actually helps. Catch you in the next one.
